events.title: Access the full richness of biological complexity with single cell and spatial multiomics
events.description: 10X Genomics is hosting a Seminar at University of Arkansas Fayetteville on May 28th. Come learn about the latest updates in single cell and spatial transcriptomics, including our newest platform: Atera!  Registration is required as lunch will be provided!   👉 Please use the registration form to confirm your attendance.   Additionally, Scientific Advisor Joe Tolar and I will be hosting office hou
events.raw_text: 10X Genomics is hosting a Seminar at University of Arkansas Fayetteville on May 28th. Come learn about the latest updates in single cell and spatial transcriptomics, including our newest platform: Atera!  Registration is required as lunch will be provided!   👉 Please use the registration form to confirm your attendance.   Additionally, Scientific Advisor Joe Tolar and I will be hosting office hours throughout the day to meet with labs and discuss any projects or anything 10X. Grab your appointment spot now! (You must go to May 28th on the calendar to see available options.)  Questions? Please reach out to Ryan.Dohn@10xgenomics.com  The vast complexities of biology require approaches to build a complete picture, starting from single cells to tissues and beyond. At 10x Genomics, we provide single cell, spatial, and in situ innovations that fuel scientific discoveries and drive exponential progress. Unravel highly complex biological systems, while bringing into focus the details that matter most. Join us to learn how Chromium Single Cell, Visium Spatial, and Atera In Situ platforms from 10x Genomics can help you push the boundaries of your research. Uncover molecular insights, dissect cell-type differences, detect novel subtypes and biomarkers, decipher spatial gene expression patterns, and more. By enabling unparalleled insights into cancer, immunology, neuroscience, and developmental biology, 10x Genomics gives researchers the ability to see biology in new ways.

events.title: Cruise to Campus
events.description: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with
events.raw_text: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with other riders, and learn about the bike resources the University of Arkansas has to offer.     This is a rain or shine event, so long as there is no lighting or thunder. If severe weather occurs, events will be postponed to a later date. For the most up to date information, check out the OFS Instagram @sustainua

events.raw_text: Join the School of Art in the Fulbright College of Arts and Sciences at the University of Arkansas for an open studio visit with internationally acclaimed artist Carla Edwards. Carla will be at the School of Art for a monthlong residency this June as part of Handwork: Celebrating American Craft 2026, a national initiative commemorating the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. The residency will culminate in a public open house from 5-7 p.m. June 23 at the Studio + Design Center with light refreshments and snacks. Visitors will have the opportunity to meet Edwards, view works in progress and engage directly with her artistic practice. The event is free and open to the public. In this webinar we’ll look at how to:  · Understand the threats  · Know how it can happen  · Secure yo
events.raw_text: Identity theft is a danger for each and every one of us—almost on a daily basis. Whether it’s an email or text that sounds enticing, a suspicious phone call or a social media post with an urgent request from a supposed “friend,” someone may be trying to steal your identity for financial gain. events.raw_text: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with other riders, and learn about the bike resources the University of Arkansas has to offer.     This is a rain or shine event, so long as there is no lighting or thunder. If severe weather occurs, events will be postponed to a later date. For the most up to date information, check out the OFS Instagram @sustainua
